title: Migrate PDS shortDescription fields from plain string to richtext object
description:
## Files
- scripts/migrations/migrate-info-shortdescription-to-richtext.js (create)
- scripts/pds/check-info-shortdescription-type.js (create)

## What to do

### Problem
All existing PDS records on climateai.org have shortDescription as a plain string (e.g. 'FLORESTAL SANTA MARIA PROJECT...'). The SDK lexicon v0.1.1 expects shortDescription to be a richtext object: { text: 'the string', facets: [] } matching the app.gainforest.common.defs#richtext schema. This causes gainforestSdk.getServerCaller().gainforest.organization.info.get() to throw UNPROCESSABLE_CONTENT for every organization.

### 1. Create check script: scripts/pds/check-info-shortdescription-type.js
Follow the exact pattern of scripts/pds/check-info-descriptions.js but instead of checking for escaped newlines, check the TYPE of shortDescription:
- For each repo, fetch the info record
- Report whether shortDescription is a string (needs migration) or an object (already migrated)
- Output results to tmp/info-shortdescription-type-check.json

### 2. Create migration script: scripts/migrations/migrate-info-shortdescription-to-richtext.js
Follow the exact pattern of scripts/migrations/normalize-info-descriptions.js (same CLI args, same loadInventory, same loadCredentials, same login flow, same dry-run support, same result logging). The ONLY difference is the transformation logic:

For each repo's info record:
1. Read the current shortDescription value
2. If it's already an object with a 'text' property, skip (already migrated)
3. If it's a string, transform it to: { text: shortDescription } (no facets needed — plain text has no rich formatting)
4. Write the updated record back with putRecord (preserving ALL other fields unchanged)

CLI interface (identical to existing migration scripts):
```
node scripts/migrations/migrate-info-shortdescription-to-richtext.js   [--service https://climateai.org]   [--repos-file tmp/pds-repo-inventory-*.json]   [--credentials tmp/pds-credentials.json]   [--limit N]   [--handle handle.climateai.org]   [--dry-run]   [--out tmp/info-shortdescription-richtext-migration-results.json]
```

### Key details
- The richtext schema is: { text: string, facets?: array } — only 'text' is required
- Do NOT add $type to the richtext object — ATProto putRecord handles that
- Preserve ALL other fields on the info record (spread the existing record, only replace shortDescription)
- Use CommonJS require() style like all other migration scripts (not ESM import)
- Use the same eslint-disable comments as existing scripts

## Don't
- Modify longDescription (it uses a different schema — linearDocument — and is a separate concern)
- Modify any existing migration scripts
- Modify any application source code
- Run the migration (just create the scripts — running is a manual step)
acceptance_criteria:
1. scripts/pds/check-info-shortdescription-type.js exists and follows the pattern of check-info-descriptions.js
2. scripts/migrations/migrate-info-shortdescription-to-richtext.js exists and follows the pattern of normalize-info-descriptions.js
3. The migration transforms string shortDescription to { text: shortDescription } richtext object
4. Already-migrated records (shortDescription is object with text property) are skipped
5. All other info record fields are preserved unchanged during migration
6. Both scripts support --dry-run, --limit, --handle, --service, --repos-file, --credentials, --out flags
7. bun run lint passes
8. bun run build passes
close_reason: eb7e61f add shortDescription richtext migration and check scripts

title: Migrate PDS longDescription fields from plain string to linearDocument object
description:
## Files
- scripts/migrations/migrate-info-longdescription-to-lineardocument.js (create)
- scripts/pds/check-info-longdescription-type.js (create)

## What to do

### Problem
Same class of bug as green_globe-21 (shortDescription). All existing PDS records on climateai.org have longDescription as a plain string. The SDK lexicon expects longDescription to be a pub.leaflet.pages.linearDocument object. This causes gainforestSdk.getServerCaller().gainforest.organization.info.get() to throw UNPROCESSABLE_CONTENT for every old organization — even though shortDescription was already migrated (green_globe-21.1). Only 1 org (Satyam5, created after the schema change) works because it has the correct linearDocument format.

The linearDocument format (verified from the working Satyam5 record):
```json
{
  "$type": "pub.leaflet.pages.linearDocument",
  "blocks": [
    {
      "$type": "pub.leaflet.pages.linearDocument#block",
      "block": {
        "$type": "pub.leaflet.blocks.text",
        "plaintext": "<the original string>"
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

### 1. Create check script: scripts/pds/check-info-longdescription-type.js
Follow the exact pattern of scripts/pds/check-info-shortdescription-type.js but check the TYPE of longDescription:
- For each repo, fetch the info record
- Report whether longDescription is a string (needs migration), an object with blocks array (already migrated), or missing
- Output results to tmp/info-longdescription-type-check.json

### 2. Create migration script: scripts/migrations/migrate-info-longdescription-to-lineardocument.js
Follow the exact pattern of scripts/migrations/migrate-info-shortdescription-to-richtext.js (same CLI args, same loadInventory, same loadCredentials, same login flow, same dry-run support, same result logging). The ONLY difference is the transformation logic:

For each repo's info record:
1. Read the current longDescription value
2. If it's already an object with a 'blocks' property, skip (already migrated)
3. If it's a string, transform it to the linearDocument format shown above
4. If it's missing/undefined, skip
5. Write the updated record back with putRecord (preserving ALL other fields unchanged)

### Key details
- The $type fields ARE required in the linearDocument — ATProto needs them for lexicon validation
- The block type is pub.leaflet.blocks.text with a 'plaintext' field (NOT 'text' — that's the richtext format)
- Preserve ALL other fields on the info record (spread the existing record, only replace longDescription)
- Use CommonJS require() style like all other migration scripts (not ESM import)
- Use the same eslint-disable comments as existing scripts

## Don't
- Modify shortDescription (already migrated)
- Modify any existing migration scripts
- Modify any application source code
- Run the migration (just create the scripts — running is a manual step)
acceptance_criteria:
1. scripts/pds/check-info-longdescription-type.js exists and follows the pattern of check-info-shortdescription-type.js
2. scripts/migrations/migrate-info-longdescription-to-lineardocument.js exists and follows the pattern of migrate-info-shortdescription-to-richtext.js
3. The migration transforms string longDescription to linearDocument object with correct $type annotations
4. The linearDocument wraps the string in a single text block with plaintext field
5. Already-migrated records (longDescription is object with blocks property) are skipped
6. Missing longDescription fields are skipped
7. All other info record fields are preserved unchanged during migration
8. Both scripts support --dry-run, --limit, --handle, --service, --repos-file, --credentials, --out flags
9. bun run lint passes
10. bun run build passes
close_reason: ffa2d4e add longDescription linearDocument migration scripts

title: Fix: visibility filter bypassed when includeInfo=false in listAllOrganizations (from green_globe-16.4)
description: Review of green_globe-16.4 found: The Public visibility filter in listAllOrganizations() only runs inside the 'if (options?.includeInfo)' block (list-all-organizations.ts line 48-63). When called with includeInfo: false (or omitted), the filter is never applied and ALL repos — including test accounts and private orgs — are returned. The route currently always passes info=true so this is not exploited today, but the utility is exported and any future caller using includeCoordinates: true without includeInfo: true will leak private org DIDs and coordinates. Evidence: list-all-organizations.ts lines 48-63 — visibility check is nested inside the includeInfo guard. Fix: move the visibility filter to a separate step that always runs when includeInfo is true, OR always fetch info to filter even when the caller doesn't want info fields in the output.
close_reason: 7f5bccd fix: always apply visibility filter in listAllOrganizations

title: Fix: response shape changed from flat to nested — spec violation in green_globe-16.5 (from green_globe-16.5)
description: Review of green_globe-16.5 found: The spec required the response shape to remain identical: Array<{ did, name?, country?, lat?, lon? }>. The new route returns Array<{ did, info: { name, country } | null, mapPoint: { lat, lon } | null }> — a nested shape. The useIndexedOrganizations hook was updated to match the new shape (it now accesses organization.info.name and organization.mapPoint.lat), so the app works. However: (1) the spec acceptance criteria AC#2 is violated — the shape is NOT identical to the old flat shape; (2) any other consumer of /api/list-organizations expecting the old flat shape would break silently. The hook import of IndexedOrganization from the route file means the type is correct, but the shape change was undocumented. Evidence: route.ts lines 15-31 (new nested type), old spec: 'Array<{ did: string; name: string; country: string; lat: number; lon: number }>'. This is a spec deviation that should be documented or the spec updated to reflect the intentional shape change.
close_reason: a76c8b3 fix: document intentional response shape change

title: Fix: no caching on /api/list-organizations — force-dynamic with 98-repo N+1 PDS calls (from green_globe-16.5)
description: Review of green_globe-16.5 found: The route has 'export const dynamic = force-dynamic' which disables all Next.js caching. Each request triggers: 1 listRepos call + up to 98 × 2 PDS getRecord calls (defaultSite + site) = ~197 sequential-ish HTTP calls to climateai.org PDS. Organization data changes infrequently (hours/days). Without caching, every page load that triggers useIndexedOrganizations will make ~197 PDS calls and likely take 3-10 seconds. The spec task 16.5 explicitly said 'Add Next.js fetch cache headers if appropriate (the data changes infrequently)'. Fix: either (a) remove force-dynamic and add export const revalidate = 300 (5 min ISR), or (b) keep force-dynamic but add Cache-Control: s-maxage=300, stale-while-revalidate=600 response header, or (c) move to a background revalidation pattern. Evidence: route.ts line 13 — force-dynamic with no cache headers.
close_reason: a76c8b3 fix: add ISR caching to list-organizations route

title: Fix: useOrganizationLayers hook is dead code — never imported by LayersOverlay store (from green_globe-20.1)
description: Review of green_globe-20.1 found: The useOrganizationLayers hook (src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_hooks/use-organization-layers.ts) was created as specified but is never imported or used anywhere. The LayersOverlay store (store/utils.ts, added in green_globe-20.2) independently re-implements the same ATProto fetching logic using ClimateAIAgent.com.atproto.repo.listRecords directly. The hook is dead code. Either: (a) the store should use the hook (but hooks can't be called from Zustand actions), or (b) the hook should be deleted since the store correctly uses the agent directly. The hook also uses trpcApi.gainforest.organization.layer.getAll.useQuery which is a React hook and cannot be called from a Zustand store action anyway — so the architectural split is correct, but the hook file is unused and misleading. Evidence: grep of all .ts/.tsx files shows use-organization-layers.ts only references itself.
close_reason: 218770a fix: remove dead useOrganizationLayers hook

title: Fix: ATProto layer records missing category/visibility mapping — legend type mismatch (from green_globe-20.2)
description:
Review of green_globe-20.2 found two related problems in normalizeAtprotoLayer (LayersOverlay/store/utils.ts and use-organization-layers.ts):

1. **legend type mismatch**: The layer lexicon defines legend as an array of legendEntry objects ({label, color, value}), but normalizeAtprotoLayer maps it as `typeof v.legend === 'string' ? v.legend : undefined` treating it as a string. The Layer type also has `legend?: string`. When ATProto layer records have a legend array, it will always be dropped (returns undefined). The Layer type and normalizer need to be updated to handle the array-of-objects format from the lexicon.

2. **visibility field does not exist in layer lexicon**: normalizeAtprotoLayer maps `v.visibility` to the `visible` field, but the layer lexicon has no `visibility` field (it has `isDefault: boolean`). The correct field to use for default visibility is `isDefault`. As a result, all ATProto layers will always have `visible: false` regardless of what the record says.

3. **Missing layer types**: The lexicon defines 3 additional type enum values (heatmap, contour, satellite_overlay) that are absent from VALID_LAYER_TYPES in both utils.ts files. Records with these types are silently downgraded to geojson_points.

Evidence: lexicons/app/gainforest/organization/layer.json lines 49-80 (category, legend as array), line 59-62 (isDefault, not visibility). normalizeAtprotoLayer in LayersOverlay/store/utils.ts lines 34-49.
close_reason: 6c19af2 fix: correct layer field mapping for legend, visibility, and types

title: Fix: fetchLayersFromS3 and fetchLayers call .json() without checking response.ok (from green_globe-20.2)
description: Review of green_globe-20.2 found: Both fetchLayersFromS3 and fetchLayers in LayersOverlay/store/utils.ts call response.json() without first checking response.ok. When S3 returns a 404 (no layerData.json for a project), the fetch succeeds at the HTTP level but returns an HTML error page. Calling .json() on that throws a SyntaxError which is caught and returns null — so the behavior is correct, but it generates a noisy console error and is fragile. The pattern should check response.ok before parsing. Evidence: utils.ts lines 62-70 (fetchLayers) and lines 122-127 (fetchLayersFromS3). Note: fetchSiteShapefile in ProjectOverlay/store/utils.ts correctly checks response.ok (line 61).
close_reason: add78d0 fix: add response.ok checks in fetchLayers and fetchLayersFromS3

title: Fix: debug console.log left in ProjectOverlay store setProjectId action (from green_globe-17.3)
description: Review of green_globe-17.3/18/19/20 found: A debug console.log statement was left in the setProjectId action of ProjectOverlay/store/index.ts (line 253): `console.log('setProjectId', projectId, navigate, zoomToSite)`. This fires on every project selection and leaks internal state (DID, navigate function reference) to the browser console in production. Evidence: src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/ProjectOverlay/store/index.ts line 253.
close_reason: c9c81e5 fix: remove debug console.log from ProjectOverlay store setProjectId

title: Fix: getBlobUrl called with profileImage as any — unsafe cast bypasses type safety (from green_globe-18.2)
description: Review of green_globe-18.2 found: In Members/index.tsx (line 73), member.profileImage is cast as `any` to satisfy getBlobUrl's SupportedImageData parameter. The AtprotoMember type declares profileImage as `unknown` (blob ref). The correct fix is to type profileImage as the actual blob ref type from the SDK (e.g., BlobRef or the ATProto blob ref shape) so the cast is unnecessary. The eslint-disable comment suppresses the warning but the underlying type is still unsafe — if profileImage is not a valid blob ref shape, getBlobUrl may throw or return a malformed URL. The try/catch around it handles the throw case, but the type should be narrowed properly. Evidence: Members/index.tsx lines 66-80, use-organization-members.ts line 16 (profileImage?: unknown).
close_reason: 9ed35fe Fix unsafe as-any cast on profileImage in getBlobUrl call

title: Fix: occurrence.json duplicates conservationStatus fields inline instead of referencing common.defs#conservationStatus (from green_globe-1.6 / green_globe-1.7)
description: Review of green_globe-1.6 and green_globe-1.7 found a cross-task seam problem. Task 1.7 defined a shared conservationStatus def in common/defs.json with fields: iucnCategory, iucnTaxonId, iucnAssessmentDate, citesAppendix, nationalStatus, nativeStatus. Task 1.6 independently added the same conservation fields inline directly on occurrence.json (iucnCategory, iucnTaxonId, iucnAssessmentDate, nativeStatus, citesAppendix, nationalConservationStatus) instead of referencing the shared def. This creates two problems: (1) 5 fields are duplicated between occurrence.json inline and common.defs#conservationStatus, wasting the shared def that task 1.7 created; (2) naming inconsistency — occurrence.json uses 'nationalConservationStatus' but common.defs#conservationStatus uses 'nationalStatus' for the same concept. The occurrence.json should either reference #conservationStatus via a ref, or the common def should be updated to match. This will cause confusion for SDK consumers and lexgen-generated types.

title: Fix: SDK-generated AppGainforestOrganizationLayer type is stale — missing category, legend, isDefault fields (from green_globe-20)
description: Review of green_globe-20 found: The gainforest-sdk's generated type for AppGainforestOrganizationLayer (Main$3 in collection-C8fSQlVi.d.ts) only includes: name, type, uri, description, createdAt. However, the lexicon (lexicons/app/gainforest/organization/layer.json) defines many additional fields: category, displayOrder, isDefault, opacity, thumbnail, legend (array), colorScale, unit, minValue, maxValue, tilePattern, tileMinZoom, tileMaxZoom, bounds, dataSource, dataDate, propertyKey, siteRef. The SDK types need to be regenerated (bun run codegen:lexicon-api) and the gainforest-sdk package needs to be updated to reflect the full lexicon. Until then, code accessing category/legend/isDefault from ATProto layer records must rely on the [k: string]: unknown index signature, which is fragile. Evidence: node_modules/gainforest-sdk/dist/collection-C8fSQlVi.d.ts lines 250-263 vs lexicons/app/gainforest/organization/layer.json.
close_reason: da6b101 fix: regenerate lexicon-api types / document SDK type limitations

title: Schema mismatch: coverImage and logo  is app.gainforest.common.defs#smallImage but lexicon expects org.hypercerts.defs#smallImage
description: Discovered during manual testing of /api/list-organizations. The SDK's gainforest.organization.info.get() rejects ~68 orgs that have coverImage and ~55 orgs that have logo fields. Root cause: PDS records store these image fields with  'app.gainforest.common.defs#smallImage' but the organization.info lexicon references 'org.hypercerts.defs#smallImage'. The type app.gainforest.common.defs#smallImage does not even exist in the common defs lexicon (it defines 'image' and 'imageThumbnail' but not 'smallImage'). Only the ~45 orgs WITHOUT coverImage currently pass SDK validation. This is the ACTIVE BLOCKER for the ATProto migration — the /api/list-organizations route returns only 20 orgs instead of ~80. Fix: migrate all coverImage and logo fields in PDS records to use the correct  'org.hypercerts.defs#smallImage'. The blob ref and structure ({image: blob}) are already correct — only the  discriminator needs updating.
close_reason: 209a9ea coverImage/logo  migrated (60/62 repos) + SDK BlobRef bypass. API returns 112 orgs.

title: Write diagnostic script: check-info-image-types.js to audit coverImage and logo  across all PDS repos
description:
## Files
- scripts/pds/check-info-image-types.js (create)

## What to do
Create a diagnostic script following the exact pattern of scripts/pds/check-info-shortdescription-type.js. The script should:

1. Accept the same CLI args: --service, --repos-file, --limit, --handle, --out
2. For each repo, fetch app.gainforest.organization.info record (rkey: 'self')
3. Check both coverImage and logo fields
4. For each field, extract the $type using Object.entries() iteration (NOT direct bracket access — the ATProto SDK makes $type inaccessible via ci['$type'] but visible via Object.entries())
5. Categorize each repo into: needs-migration (wrong $type), already-correct (org.hypercerts.defs#smallImage), no-field (field absent), or error
6. Write JSON results to --out (default: tmp/info-image-types-check.json)

Key implementation detail: To read $type from ATProto SDK objects, you MUST iterate with Object.entries() or Object.keys() + forEach. Direct property access val.coverImage['$type'] returns undefined due to SDK internalization.

Expected $type values:
- WRONG: 'app.gainforest.common.defs#smallImage' (needs migration)
- CORRECT: 'org.hypercerts.defs#smallImage' (already migrated)
- Also report any other unexpected $type values

## Don't

title: Migrate PDS coverImage and logo  from app.gainforest.common.defs#smallImage to org.hypercerts.defs#smallImage
description:
## Files
- scripts/migrations/migrate-info-image-types.js (create)

## What to do
Create a migration script following the exact pattern of scripts/migrations/migrate-info-shortdescription-to-richtext.js. The script should:

1. Accept the same CLI args: --service, --repos-file, --credentials, --limit, --handle, --dry-run, --out
2. For each repo, authenticate with credentials, fetch the info record
3. Check both coverImage and logo fields for wrong $type
4. For each field that has $type 'app.gainforest.common.defs#smallImage', replace it with 'org.hypercerts.defs#smallImage'
5. The blob ref structure ({image: {ref, mimeType, size}}) stays UNCHANGED — only the $type discriminator changes
6. Use putRecord to write the updated record back
7. Write JSON results to --out (default: tmp/info-image-types-migration-results.json)

CRITICAL implementation details:

### Reading $type from ATProto SDK objects
The ATProto SDK internalizes $type — direct bracket access returns undefined. To read it:
```js
function getType(obj) {
  for (const [k, v] of Object.entries(obj)) {
    if (k === '$type') return v
  }
  return undefined
}
```

### Writing $type back
When constructing the updated record for putRecord, you CAN set $type normally:
```js
const updatedCoverImage = { ...spreadExistingFields, '$type': 'org.hypercerts.defs#smallImage' }
```
But since spread won't copy the internalized $type, you need to manually reconstruct:
```js
const updatedCoverImage = {
  '$type': 'org.hypercerts.defs#smallImage',
  image: existingCoverImage.image  // blob ref stays the same
}
```

### Both fields need migration
Process BOTH coverImage AND logo in a single pass per repo. Only call putRecord once per repo with both fields updated.

### Skip conditions
- Skip if field is absent (no coverImage / no logo)
- Skip if $type is already 'org.hypercerts.defs#smallImage'
- Skip if no credentials for the repo
- Report each skip reason in results

### Expected scope
- ~68 repos have coverImage needing migration
- ~55 repos have logo needing migration
- ~12 repos will be skipped (no credentials)
- 2 test accounts may fail login (stale passwords)

## Don't
- Don't modify the blob ref (image field) — only change $type
- Don't remove any other fields from the record — spread the full existing record
- Don't skip logo if coverImage is absent (or vice versa) — check each independently
- Don't use 'in' operator or direct bracket access for reading $type
acceptance_criteria:
1. Script runs in dry-run mode without errors: node scripts/migrations/migrate-info-image-types.js --dry-run --limit 5
2. Dry-run output correctly identifies repos needing migration vs already-correct
3. Script follows same CLI arg pattern as migrate-info-shortdescription-to-richtext.js
4. Both coverImage and logo are handled in a single script
5. The blob ref (image field) is preserved unchanged — only $type changes
6. Results JSON includes per-repo status for both coverImage and logo
7. bun run lint passes

title: SDK BlobRef instanceof fails: Next.js webpack deduplicates @atproto/lexicon causing cross-version BlobRef mismatch
description: Root cause analysis: The gainforest-sdk bundles @atproto/api@0.18.8 which depends on @atproto/lexicon@0.6.1. The project has @atproto/api@0.17.2 with @atproto/lexicon@0.5.1. When Next.js webpack bundles server code, it may resolve the SDK's internal require('@atproto/lexicon') to the project's copy (v0.5.1) instead of the SDK's nested copy (v0.6.1). This causes the BlobRef class used for validation (from lexicon@0.5.1) to differ from the BlobRef class used for deserialization (from the SDK's api@0.18.8 which uses lexicon@0.6.1). The instanceof check in the blob validator fails, causing 'Record/coverImage/image should be a blob ref' for all orgs with coverImage or logo. This affects ~68 orgs. The  migration (green_globe-31.2) was necessary but not sufficient — the data is now correct but the SDK still can't validate it due to this bundling issue. Fix: bypass SDK validation for info.get() in listAllOrganizations by using raw ATProto agent + local Zod validation instead of the SDK's tRPC server caller.

title: Fix: Country flag emoji not showing in search list
description: ## Files - src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/Overlay/SearchOverlay/index.tsx (modify) ## What to do Country flag emojis are not visible in the search list. Two issues: ### Issue 1: Windows does not render Unicode flag emojis The countryToEmoji lookup returns emoji characters (e.g. the Brazilian flag emoji) which Windows does not render — they show as empty or two-letter pairs. The countryToEmoji entries have an `image` field with SVG URLs (e.g. `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/country-flag-emoji-json@2.0.0/dist/images/BR.svg`). Wrap the badge in a conditional: `{countryDetails && (...)}`. ### Issue 3: Case sensitivity The lookup `Object.keys(countryToEmoji).includes(info.country)` is case-sensitive. The countryToEmoji keys are uppercase ISO alpha-2 codes. Normalize with `info.country?.toUpperCase()` before lookup. ### Implementation 1. Normalize: `const countryCode = info.country?.toUpperCase()` 2. Lookup: `const countryDetails = countryCode ? countryToEmoji[countryCode] : null` 3. Render flag as SVG image: `` 4. Conditionally render the badge: only show when countryDetails is not null ### Reference Check how the Header component in ProjectOverlay renders country — it already conditionally renders with `{countryDetails && (...)}`. Follow the same pattern. ## Don't - Don't change the countryToEmoji data source - Don't modify the API response shape - Don't remove the country name text — keep both flag image and country name

title: Epic: Migrate community member data from PostgreSQL to PDS
description:
## Why
The Community tab shows empty for all organizations because 0/146 repos have member records on the PDS. The frontend (green_globe-18) was already migrated to read from ATProto, but no member data was ever written to the PDS. The source data is in a PostgreSQL database (Supabase backup at db_cluster-28-04-2025@14-59-40.backup) containing a public.User table (~103 rows) with first_name, last_name, title, bio, profile_image_url, display_order, and a project_id hash that maps to the Project table's id column. A separate public.Wallet table maps user_id -> blockchain wallet addresses (chain + address).

## What Success Looks Like
- All ~103 members from the PostgreSQL User table have corresponding app.gainforest.organization.member records on the PDS
- Each member record includes: displayName, firstName, lastName, role (from title), bio, displayOrder, walletAddresses (from Wallet table join), profileImage (downloaded from S3 and uploaded as blob where profile_image_url exists)
- The Community tab in the UI shows members for organizations that have them
- A dry-run mode exists for validation before live execution

## Key Constraints
- The User table's project_id is a SHA-256 hash of the project name (see dry-run-sql.js slugify pattern). Must map project_id hash -> PDS handle/DID
- Profile images are on S3 (e.g. community/james-thembo.jpg) — need to download and upload as PDS blobs
- The member lexicon requires: displayName (required), role (required), did (required — use org DID as placeholder), createdAt (required)
- Wallet table has duplicate rows (same user_id, same address) — must deduplicate
- 14 orgs lack PDS credentials — those will be skipped
- Follow existing migration script patterns (--service, --repos-file, --credentials, --dry-run, --out flags)

title: Build project_id-to-PDS-handle mapping from SQL dump
description:
## Files
- scripts/migrations/migrate-members-to-pds.js (create)

## What to do
Create the first part of the member migration script that builds a mapping from the PostgreSQL project_id hash to PDS handles/DIDs.

1. Parse the SQL dump file (path passed via --sql-dump arg, default: look for *.backup or *.sql in parent directories) to extract the Project table rows. Reuse the COPY parser pattern from scripts/migrations/dry-run-sql.js (parseCopyRows function).
2. For each Project row, compute the project_id hash the same way the original app did: SHA-256 of the project name string (the id column in the COPY data IS the hash already — it's a 64-char hex string).
3. Load the PDS repo inventory (tmp/pds-repo-inventory-*.json) to get handle->DID mappings.
4. Match Project rows to PDS handles by slugifying the Project name and comparing to the handle prefix (handle minus .climateai.org). Use the same slug matching strategy as upsert-predictions-observations.js (exact match, prefix match, override map).
5. Output a JSON mapping: { projectIdHash: { handle, did, projectName } }
6. Also parse the User table rows and group them by project_id.
7. Parse the Wallet table rows and index by user_id (deduplicate: same user_id + same address = keep one).

The script should accept these flags following the existing pattern:
--sql-dump <path>, --service <url>, --repos-file <path>, --credentials <path>, --dry-run, --out <path>, --limit <N>, --handle <handle>

title: Epic: Migrate project-specific layer configs from S3 to PDS
description:
## Why
Only 2/146 repos have layer records on the PDS (both test accounts). Every real organization falls back to S3 for layer configuration. The frontend (green_globe-20) already reads from ATProto first with S3 fallback, but since no real orgs have layer records, every org hits S3. We need a migration script to read layerData.json files from S3 and create app.gainforest.organization.layer records on the PDS.

## What Success Looks Like
- All organizations that have layerData.json on S3 have corresponding layer records on the PDS
- The frontend loads layers from ATProto instead of falling back to S3
- Global layers remain on S3 (deliberate decision — no ATProto path for global layers)
- A dry-run mode exists for validation before live execution

## Key Constraints
- Layer data source: https://gainforest-transparency-dashboard.s3.amazonaws.com/layers/{kebab-slug}/layerData.json (public, no AWS credentials needed)
- The kebab-slug may not exactly match the PDS handle prefix — need flexible slug matching (same strategy as predictions migration)
- Layer records are pure metadata/config (~300-800 bytes each). The actual raster/vector data stays on S3 — the layer record's uri field points to the S3 data URL
- The layer lexicon supports 10 types: geojson_points, geojson_points_trees, geojson_line, choropleth, choropleth_shannon, raster_tif, tms_tile, heatmap, contour, satellite_overlay
- SDK-generated types for layers are stale (missing category, legend, isDefault) — the code uses [k: string]: unknown workaround (green_globe-30)
- 14 orgs lack PDS credentials — those will be skipped
- Global layers (/layers/global/layerData.json) are NOT migrated — they stay on S3

## Scope
- Only project-specific layers (not global)
- Download layerData.json from S3, parse, create PDS records
- Follow existing migration script patterns

title: Write migrate-layers-to-pds.js — download S3 layerData.json and create PDS layer records
description:
## Files
- scripts/migrations/migrate-layers-to-pds.js (create)

## What to do
Create a migration script that reads layerData.json from the LOCAL directory (downloaded from S3) for each organization and creates app.gainforest.organization.layer records on the PDS.

### Step 1: Build org slug index
1. Load PDS repo inventory (tmp/pds-repo-inventory-*.json) for handle->DID mappings
2. For each handle, derive the slug (handle minus .climateai.org)
3. Use the same SLUG_OVERRIDES map from upsert-predictions-observations.js for truncated handles (e.g. oceanus-conservati -> oceanus-conservation)

### Step 2: Read layerData.json from local directory
The layer data has been downloaded to 'S3 Layer Data/' in the project root. Accept a --local-dir flag (default: 'S3 Layer Data').
For each subdirectory in local-dir (excluding 'global'):
1. Read {slug}/layerData.json
2. Match the slug to a PDS handle/DID using the slug index from Step 1
3. Parse the JSON which has shape: { layers: Array<{ name, type, endpoint, description?, category?, legend?, visible? }> }

There are exactly 6 project-specific orgs with layer data:
- inhaa-be (1 layer)
- oceanus-conservation (15 layers)
- parque-das-tribos (unknown count)
- toca-do-tatu (unknown count)
- tumbira (unknown count)
- xprize-rainforest-finals (18 layers)

### Step 3: Create PDS layer records
For each layer in the layerData.json:
1. Login to the org's PDS account using credentials from tmp/pds-credentials.json
2. Map the S3 layer object to the PDS lexicon:
   - name -> name (required, max 256 graphemes)
   - type -> type (must be one of the 10 known values in the lexicon)
   - endpoint -> uri (STORE AS-IS including template literals like ${process.env.TITILER_ENDPOINT} and ${process.env.AWS_STORAGE} — the frontend already resolves these at runtime via the existing layer store code)
   - description -> description (max 2000 graphemes)
   - category -> category (max 128 graphemes)
   - legend -> legend (array of { label, color, value? }) if present
   - visible/isDefault -> isDefault (boolean) if present
   - createdAt -> new Date().toISOString()
3. Create via agent.com.atproto.repo.createRecord({ repo: did, collection: 'app.gainforest.organization.layer', record })

### CLI flags (standard pattern from other migration scripts):
--service <url> (default: https://climateai.org), --repos-file <path>, --credentials <path>, --local-dir <path> (default: 'S3 Layer Data'), --dry-run, --out <path>, --limit <N>, --handle <handle>

### Reference files to study for patterns:
- scripts/migrations/upsert-predictions-observations.js (SLUG_OVERRIDES, parseArgs, loadInventory, login pattern)
- scripts/migrations/normalize-info-descriptions.js (same CLI pattern)
- lexicons/app/gainforest/organization/layer.json (the lexicon schema)

## Don't
- Migrate global layers (skip the 'global' subdirectory)
- Modify any frontend files
- Download anything from S3 — data is already local
- Delete existing layer records on the PDS
- Resolve the template literals in endpoint strings — store them as-is
acceptance_criteria:
1. scripts/migrations/migrate-layers-to-pds.js exists and runs without error
2. Dry-run produces a JSON report showing per-org layer counts and layer names
3. The script correctly fetches layerData.json from S3 for orgs that have it
4. Layer records are created with correct type enum values
5. The endpoint field is stored as a full URL (not relative path)
6. Legend arrays are preserved correctly
7. Orgs without layerData.json on S3 are skipped gracefully (not errors)
8. bun run lint passes

title: Epic: Migrate biodiversity predictions to PDS and update frontend
description:
## Why
Plants/animals biodiversity predictions still read from S3 Restor JSON files. The PDS has deprecated prediction/observation collections that only store GBIF taxon keys (not the rich data the UI needs). The proper replacement lexicon app.gainforest.dwc.occurrence already exists and models everything: scientificName, IUCN status, plant traits, images, native/invasive classification, taxonomy.

A previous Codex CLI session (see tmp/Biodiversity data migration with GBIF key resolution.md) built the GBIF-key-only migration script (scripts/migrations/upsert-predictions-observations.js) with slug matching and overrides. Dry run succeeded (41 upserts across 33 orgs) but the live run was NEVER executed.

## Strategy: Both Path A and Path B
- **Path A (GBIF keys):** Run the existing migration script live to populate the deprecated predictions/observations collections with GBIF taxon keys. This provides a baseline index.
- **Path B (Rich DWC occurrence records):** Write a NEW migration script that creates app.gainforest.dwc.occurrence records with ALL the rich data from the local Restor JSON files (scientificName, iucnCategory, traits, images, edibleParts, native/invasive group). Then update the frontend to read from PDS instead of S3.
- **The UI will use Path B** — reading from dwc.occurrence records.

## What Success Looks Like
- Path A: deprecated prediction/observation collections populated with GBIF keys (33 orgs)
- Path B: dwc.occurrence records created with full species data for all orgs with Restor data
- Frontend Predictions component reads from PDS dwc.occurrence records instead of S3
- S3 fallback remains for orgs without PDS data

## Key Constraints
- Local data at: S3 Observations and Predictions Data/restor/ (trees/herbs JSON with rich species data)
- Local data at: S3 Observations and Predictions Data/predictions/ (fauna CSV)
- Local data at: S3 Observations and Predictions Data/observations/ (DWC CSV)
- GBIF cache at: tmp/gbif-cache.json (5224 lines of species->key mappings)
- 14 orgs lack PDS credentials
- The dwc.occurrence lexicon key is tid (one record per species per org)
- Frontend types: BiodiversityPlant (key, scientificName, commonName, iucnCategory, iucnTaxonId, group, edibleParts, awsUrl, imageUrl, traits) and BiodiversityAnimal (Species, Type)

title: Path A: Run existing predictions migration script live and add GBIF match fallback
description:
## Files

- tmp/gbif-cache.json (modify — clear null entries for retry)

## What to do
1. Add a GBIF 'match' API fallback to the existing script. Currently it only uses species/search. Add a second attempt using https://api.gbif.org/v1/species/match?name={name} for species that returned null from the search endpoint. This should resolve several of the 12 unresolved species (Licaria cannella, Cestrum strigilatum, Psydrax livida, etc.).
2. For 'Genus sp.' entries (e.g. 'Bellucia sp.', 'Ficus sp.'), map to the genus-level taxon key by stripping ' sp.' and querying GBIF for the genus. Store these with a flag or just accept genus-level keys.
3. Clear null entries from tmp/gbif-cache.json before re-running so the new fallback gets a chance.
4. Re-run dry-run to verify improved resolution: bun run migrate:pred-obs -- --dry-run --local-dir 'S3 Observations and Predictions Data' --out tmp/pred-obs-migration-results-dry-v2.json
5. If dry-run looks good, this task is complete. The actual live run will be done by the user.

## Don't
- Run the live migration (user will do that)
- Modify any frontend files
- Change the PDS collection names or record schema
acceptance_criteria:
1. GBIF match fallback is implemented as a second attempt after search fails
2. Genus sp. entries are resolved to genus-level taxon keys
3. Null entries in gbif-cache.json are cleared before retry
4. Dry-run shows fewer unresolved species than the previous 12
5. Dry-run still shows 41+ upserts (should not decrease)
6. bun run lint passes

title: Path B: Write migrate-dwc-occurrences.js — create rich DWC occurrence records from Restor data
description:
## Files
- scripts/migrations/migrate-dwc-occurrences.js (create)

## What to do
Create a NEW migration script that reads the rich Restor JSON data (trees/herbs) and fauna CSV data, and creates app.gainforest.dwc.occurrence records on the PDS with ALL the fields the frontend needs.

### For each org with Restor data (S3 Observations and Predictions Data/restor/):
1. Load {slug}-trees.json and {slug}-herbs.json
2. For each species item in the items[] array, create a dwc.occurrence record:
   - scientificName: item.scientificName (required)
   - gbifTaxonKey: look up from tmp/gbif-cache.json using scientificName, or resolve via GBIF API
   - basisOfRecord: 'MachineObservation' (these are Restor model predictions)
   - eventDate: new Date().toISOString() (required)
   - createdAt: new Date().toISOString() (required)
   - conservationStatus: { $type: 'app.gainforest.common.defs#conservationStatus', iucnCategory: item.iucnCategory, iucnTaxonId: String(item.iucnTaxonId) } (if present)
   - plantTraits: { $type: 'app.gainforest.dwc.defs#plantTraits', woodDensity: item.traits.woodDensity, maxHeight: item.traits.treeHeight, stemDiameter: item.traits.stemDiameter, stemConduitDiameter: item.traits.stemConduitDiameter, barkThickness: item.traits.barkThickness, rootDepth: item.traits.rootDepth, edibleParts: item.edibleParts } (if traits present)
   - speciesImageUrl: item.awsUrl or item.imageUrl (if present)
   - thumbnailUrl: item.awsUrl or item.imageUrl (if present)
   - occurrenceID: item.key (e.g. 'abarema_cochleata')
   - kingdom: 'Plantae'
   - dynamicProperties: JSON.stringify({ group: item.group, source: 'restor', dataType: herbs ? 'herbs' : 'trees' })

### For fauna predictions (S3 Observations and Predictions Data/predictions/{slug}.csv):
   - scientificName: Species column
   - basisOfRecord: 'MachineObservation'
   - kingdom: 'Animalia'
   - class or dynamicProperties: { animalType: Type column (Reptile/Amphibian/Mammal/Bird) }
   - gbifTaxonKey: from cache or GBIF API

### Slug matching:
Reuse the same SLUG_OVERRIDES and matching logic from upsert-predictions-observations.js.

### CLI flags (standard pattern):
--service, --repos-file, --credentials, --local-dir, --dry-run, --out, --limit, --handle

- Modify any frontend files (separate task)

title: Epic: Fix trees not showing for Oceanus Conservation and other truncated-handle orgs
description:
## Problem
Trees are not showing when clicking on 'Oceanus Conservation' (did:plc:6oxtzu7gxz7xcldvtwfh3bpt, handle: oceanus-conservati.climateai.org) on the globe.

## Root Cause Analysis
Two compounding issues prevent trees from displaying:

### Issue 1: Wrong blob CID on PDS site records (PRIMARY)
During tree data migration, all 5 Oceanus Conservation site records were updated with blob CID bafkreiclcaikdxi7nrls45gllljlzcjmgzvmv2pnh43xpcuhqbhmacwqvu (187 bytes, empty features array). The correct 4.1MB blob with actual tree data (bafkreibghofvcze46eh6p3x3oryq5iel4g4z4nnzhdooumub4milzs2d3y) was uploaded but never referenced by any site record (last-write-wins during migration overwrote correct refs with empty ones). The correct blob is likely garbage-collected.

### Issue 2: S3 fallback uses truncated slug (SECONDARY)
Even if the PDS blob fetch failed, the S3 fallback would construct the wrong URL. fetchOrganizationSlug() derives slug from handle.split('.')[0] = 'oceanus-conservati' (truncated by PDS to 18 chars), but the S3 file is named 'oceanus-conservation-all-tree-plantings.geojson'. Migration scripts have a SLUG_OVERRIDES map for this, but the frontend does not.

### Issue 3: No empty-features guard (DEFENSIVE)
fetchMeasuredTreesShapefile() in store/utils.ts treats any 200 OK response as success, even if features array is empty. It should fall through to S3 fallback when blob returns empty features.

## Success Criteria
- Trees display correctly when clicking Oceanus Conservation on the globe
- S3 fallback works for all orgs with truncated handles (18+ char names)
- Empty blob responses trigger fallback instead of silently showing nothing

## Scope
- Frontend code fix (slug overrides + empty-features guard)
- PDS data fix (re-upload correct tree blob for Oceanus Conservation)
- Affects: src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/ProjectOverlay/store/

title: Epic: Migrate images from S3/external sources to ATProto PDS
description:
## Goal
Migrate tree measurement photos and species occurrence images from AWS S3 (and other external sources) to the ATProto PDS at climateai.org, using dwc.occurrence as the unified record type for all biodiversity data.

## Context
- 27,821 tree photos on S3 (107.5 GB raw), ~15,147 referenced in GeoJSON files across 91 org GeoJSON files
- 3,477 species/Restor files on S3 (686 MB) with species image URLs in dwc.occurrence records
- Tree photos currently embedded as URL strings inside GeoJSON blobs on site records (undeclared 'trees' field)
- Species images stored as speciesImageUrl/thumbnailUrl strings on dwc.occurrence, imageEvidence blob field exists but is never populated
- Airtable dependency already removed (prior epic green_globe-16)

## Key Decisions (locked in)
1. Use dwc.occurrence for measured trees (basisOfRecord: HumanObservation) — NOT a new measuredTree lexicon
2. Add trunkEvidence, leafEvidence, barkEvidence fields to dwc.occurrence lexicon (app.gainforest.common.defs#image)
3. Discriminate measured trees via basisOfRecord (HumanObservation vs MachineObservation) + dynamicProperties.dataType
4. Compress images to 800px wide, WebP quality 80 (~340KB avg) for PDS; keep full-res on S3
5. Populate imageEvidence blob on species occurrences, then remove speciesImageUrl/thumbnailUrl string fields
6. Each tree = 1 dwc.occurrence + N dwc.measurement records for DBH/height
7. Migration scripts must be idempotent and resumable, streaming one-at-a-time (download → compress → upload → delete local)
8. Deprecate measuredTreesCluster lexicon and undeclared trees field on site records
9. KoboToolbox work deferred until tree photo architecture is settled
10. Animals have zero images (predictions only) — no image migration needed for fauna

## Scope
- Lexicon changes to dwc.occurrence (add 3 image fields)
- Migration script for measured tree photos (S3 → PDS blobs + new dwc.occurrence/measurement records)
- Migration script for species occurrence images (download from source URLs → compress → upload as imageEvidence blobs)
- Frontend updates to read measured trees from dwc.occurrence instead of GeoJSON blobs
- Frontend updates to read imageEvidence blobs instead of speciesImageUrl strings
- Cleanup: deprecate old data paths, fix existing bugs (cover image URL case, next.config wildcard)

## Out of Scope
- KoboToolbox photo migration (deferred)
- Google Drive photos (Oceanus Conservation — needs separate access investigation)
- Animal species images (none exist)
- Map tile imagery (Mapbox, Planet, ESA Terrascope — third-party services)
- Layer thumbnails (separate effort)
- Audio recordings/spectrograms (separate effort)

## Success Criteria
- All referenced tree photos (~6,449 from S3) uploaded as compressed blobs on dwc.occurrence records
- All species occurrence images uploaded as imageEvidence blobs
- Frontend displays tree photos and species images from PDS blobs
- No S3 image URLs remain in active frontend code paths (S3 kept as fallback only during transition)
- speciesImageUrl/thumbnailUrl fields deprecated on occurrence records

```json
"trunkEvidence": {
  "type": "ref",
  "ref": "app.gainforest.common.defs#image",
  "description": "Image of the trunk or whole organism. Used for measured tree records (basisOfRecord: HumanObservation). Compressed to 800px wide WebP on PDS; full-res kept on external storage."
```

3. No blob exists in temporary storage for more than a few seconds without being referenced
4. If the script is killed between photo uploads, already-uploaded photos are safe (referenced by the record)
5. On resume, partially-migrated records get their missing photos added
6. Script still passes all original acceptance criteria from 40.3
7. `bun run lint` passes

title: Redo 40.4: Write species occurrence image migration script with atomic blob referencing
description:
## Files
- scripts/migrations/migrate-occurrence-images-to-blobs.js (create)

## Context
The previous attempt at this task (green_globe-40.4) was reported as complete but the code file was never committed to git. This task must be redone from scratch.

CRITICAL: The ATProto blob spec requires that uploaded blobs be referenced by a record before the PDS garbage collects them. The pattern MUST be: upload blob → immediately putRecord to reference it. See `atproto-docs/specs/blobs.md` lines 27-37.

## What to do
Create an idempotent, resumable migration script that populates the `imageEvidence` blob field on existing `dwc.occurrence` records that currently only have `speciesImageUrl`/`thumbnailUrl` string fields.

### For each organization DID
1. List all dwc.occurrence records via `com.atproto.repo.listRecords({ collection: 'app.gainforest.dwc.occurrence' })`
2. Filter to records where:
   - `basisOfRecord === 'MachineObservation'` (species predictions, not measured trees)
   - `speciesImageUrl` or `thumbnailUrl` is a non-empty string URL
   - `imageEvidence` is NOT already populated (skip already-migrated records)

### For each matching record — ATOMIC BLOB REFERENCING
1. Download the image from the `speciesImageUrl` (or `thumbnailUrl` fallback)
2. Compress using sharp: resize to 800px wide (withoutEnlargement: true), convert to WebP quality 80
3. Upload via `agent.uploadBlob(buffer, { encoding: 'image/webp' })`
4. **IMMEDIATELY** update the record via `putRecord` to add `imageEvidence: { $type: 'app.gainforest.common.defs#image', file: blobRef }`
   - The putRecord call MUST happen immediately after uploadBlob — no other operations in between
   - This ensures the blob is referenced before the PDS can garbage collect it
   - Preserve ALL existing fields on the record (spread the existing record value, then add imageEvidence)
5. Keep `speciesImageUrl` and `thumbnailUrl` on the record for now (removal is a separate task)

### Preserving existing record fields
When calling putRecord, you MUST preserve all existing fields:
```javascript
const existingRecord = await agent.com.atproto.repo.getRecord({
  repo: did,
  collection: DWC_OCCURRENCE_COLLECTION,
  rkey: rkey
})
const updatedRecord = {
  ...existingRecord.data.value,
  imageEvidence: { $type: 'app.gainforest.common.defs#image', file: blobRef }
}
await agent.com.atproto.repo.putRecord({
  repo: did,
  collection: DWC_OCCURRENCE_COLLECTION,
  rkey: rkey,
  record: updatedRecord
})
```

### Resumability
- Progress file at `scripts/migrations/data/occurrence-images-progress.json`
- Track: `{ orgDid: { completedRkeys: string[] } }`
- On restart, skip completed rkeys

### Error handling
- Log failures to `scripts/migrations/data/occurrence-images-errors.json`
- Some image URLs may be dead (404) — log and skip, don't fail
- Retry downloads up to 3 times with exponential backoff
- Continue on individual record failure

### Auth
Same pattern as `migrate-members-to-pds.js`: reads PDS credentials from `tmp/pds-credentials.json`, uses `--service`, `--handle`, `--limit`, `--dry-run` flags.

### Reference
- Follow the same CLI arg pattern as `migrate-measured-trees-to-dwc-occurrences.js`
- Follow the atomic blob pattern from `upload-and-update-site-cover-images-atomic.js`

## Don't
- Don't create new occurrence records (only update existing ones)
- Don't remove speciesImageUrl/thumbnailUrl fields yet (backward compat during transition)
- Don't process records that already have imageEvidence populated
- Don't modify any frontend code or lexicon files
- Don't leave uploaded blobs unreferenced — every uploadBlob MUST be followed immediately by putRecord
acceptance_criteria:
1. Script file exists at scripts/migrations/migrate-occurrence-images-to-blobs.js
2. Script runs without crashing on at least one org's occurrence data (--dry-run mode)
3. Each uploadBlob call is IMMEDIATELY followed by a putRecord that references the blob
4. Records with speciesImageUrl now also have imageEvidence blob ref populated
5. Compressed images are 800px wide WebP at quality 80
6. speciesImageUrl and thumbnailUrl fields are preserved (not removed)
7. Records that already had imageEvidence are skipped (idempotent)
8. Progress file enables resumability
9. Dead URLs (404) are logged and skipped gracefully
10. Running twice produces identical PDS state
11. All existing record fields are preserved when updating (no field loss)
12. `bun run lint` passes
close_reason: bc03814 Write species occurrence image migration script with atomic blob referencing

title: Write migration script: measured tree photos from S3 to PDS as dwc.occurrence records
description:
## Files
- scripts/migrations/migrate-measured-trees-to-dwc-occurrences.js (create)

## What to do
Create an idempotent, resumable migration script that:

### Phase 1: For each organization with tree GeoJSON data
1. List all site records for the org via `com.atproto.repo.listRecords({ collection: 'app.gainforest.organization.site' })`
2. For each site with a `trees` blob ref, fetch the GeoJSON blob via `com.atproto.sync.getBlob`
3. If no blob ref, fall back to S3: `https://gainforest-transparency-dashboard.s3.amazonaws.com/shapefiles/{slug}-all-tree-plantings.geojson`

### Phase 2: For each tree feature in the GeoJSON
1. Check if a dwc.occurrence already exists for this tree (idempotency — use occurrenceID as dedup key, constructed from org DID + tree coordinates + species)
2. If not, create a `dwc.occurrence` record with:
   - `basisOfRecord: 'HumanObservation'`
   - `kingdom: 'Plantae'`
   - `scientificName`: from feature properties (`species`, `Plant_Name`, or `scientificName`)
   - `eventDate`: from `dateMeasured` property (ISO 8601) or current date as fallback
   - `decimalLatitude`, `decimalLongitude`: from feature geometry coordinates
   - `siteRef`: AT-URI of the site record
   - `dynamicProperties`: JSON string with `{"source":"field","dataType":"measuredTree","originalAwsUrl":"...","originalKoboUrl":"..."}`
   - `associatedMedia`: pipe-delimited original S3/Kobo URLs (for full-res reference)
   - `createdAt`: current ISO datetime

### Phase 3: Upload compressed photos as blobs
For each photo URL on the tree feature (awsUrl, leafAwsUrl, barkAwsUrl — falling back to koboUrl variants):
1. Download the image from S3/Kobo
2. Compress using sharp: resize to 800px wide (withoutEnlargement: true), convert to WebP quality 80
3. Upload via `agent.uploadBlob(buffer, { encoding: 'image/webp' })`
4. Set the blob ref on the occurrence record:
   - awsUrl/koboUrl → `trunkEvidence: { $type: 'app.gainforest.common.defs#image', file: blobRef }`
   - leafAwsUrl/leafKoboUrl → `leafEvidence: { ... }`
   - barkAwsUrl/barkKoboUrl → `barkEvidence: { ... }`
5. Delete the local temp file immediately after upload

### Phase 4: Create dwc.measurement records
For each tree with DBH or height data:
1. Create `dwc.measurement` record with `measurementType: 'DBH'`, `measurementValue`, `measurementUnit: 'cm'`, linked via `occurrenceRef`
2. Create `dwc.measurement` record with `measurementType: 'height'`, `measurementValue`, `measurementUnit: 'm'`, linked via `occurrenceRef`

### Resumability
- Maintain a progress file at `scripts/migrations/data/measured-trees-progress.json` tracking: `{ orgDid: { siteRkey: { lastProcessedFeatureIndex: N, completedFeatures: [...] } } }`
- On restart, skip already-completed features
- Use `putRecord` (not `createRecord`) with a deterministic rkey derived from the tree's unique identifier to ensure idempotency

### Error handling
- Log failures to `scripts/migrations/data/measured-trees-errors.json`
- Continue processing other trees on individual failure
- Retry failed image downloads up to 3 times with exponential backoff

### Special cases
- Oceanus Conservation uses `FCD-tree_records-tree_photo` (Google Drive URLs) — log these as skipped with reason 'google-drive-url', do not attempt download
- Ayyoweca Uganda uses `media_sources.images[]` format — handle via the same converter logic in `ayyoweca-uganda.ts`
- Some GeoJSON files have 51-byte empty files on S3 — skip these gracefully
- Koko DAO has the same 189 trees duplicated across 9 site GeoJSONs — the deterministic rkey ensures dedup

### Dependencies
- Requires: `@atproto/api`, `sharp`, `csv-parse` (already in package.json)
- Auth: uses PDS_ENDPOINT and PDS credentials from .env (same pattern as existing migration scripts like `migrate-members-to-pds.js`)

## Don't
- Don't modify any frontend code
- Don't modify any lexicon files
- Don't delete or modify existing PDS records (only create new ones)
- Don't download all images at once — stream one at a time to stay within 12GB disk
- Don't process Google Drive URLs (Oceanus) — just log them as skipped
acceptance_criteria:
1. Script runs without crashing on at least one org's tree data
2. Creates dwc.occurrence records with basisOfRecord 'HumanObservation' and dynamicProperties containing dataType 'measuredTree'
3. Compressed images are 800px wide WebP at quality 80
4. Each occurrence has trunkEvidence/leafEvidence/barkEvidence blob refs where photos exist
5. dwc.measurement records are created for DBH and height where data exists
6. Progress file is written and script can resume from where it left off
7. Errors are logged to error file, not swallowed silently
8. No more than one image file exists on disk at any time during execution
9. Google Drive URLs (Oceanus) are logged as skipped, not attempted
10. Running the script twice produces no duplicate records (idempotent via deterministic rkeys)
close_reason: bc03814 Write migration script: measured tree photos from S3 to PDS as dwc.occurrence records

title: Fix 40.5: getTreePhotos placeholder guard is dead code — PDS trees always show awsUrl, never placeholder
Fix 40.5: fetchPlantsFromATProto includes HumanObservation measured trees in species predictions panel
description:
## Review of green_globe-40.5 found: measured tree records bleed into biodiversity predictions panel

`fetchPlantsFromATProto` in `src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/ProjectOverlay/Biodiversity/Predictions/store/utils.ts` (lines 270-315) filters only by `kingdom === 'Plantae'`. It does NOT filter by `basisOfRecord`.

After green_globe-40.5 lands, migrated orgs will have `HumanObservation` dwc.occurrence records (measured trees) in their collection alongside `MachineObservation` species prediction records. Both have `kingdom: 'Plantae'`. `fetchPlantsFromATProto` will therefore include measured trees in the species predictions panel — showing individual field-measured trees as if they were species predictions, with trunk photo URLs as species images.

## Evidence
- `Predictions/store/utils.ts` line 293: `if (kingdom !== 'Plantae') continue;` — no basisOfRecord check
- `use-organization-measured-trees.ts` line 241: measured trees are `basisOfRecord === 'HumanObservation'`
- `Predictions/store/index.ts` line 74: calls `fetchPlantsFromATProto(did)` with no filter

## Fix
Add a `basisOfRecord !== 'HumanObservation'` guard inside the loop in `fetchPlantsFromATProto`:

```ts
// Skip measured trees — they are HumanObservation records, not species predictions
const basisOfRecord =
  typeof record.value.basisOfRecord === 'string'
    ? record.value.basisOfRecord
    : undefined;
if (basisOfRecord === 'HumanObservation') continue;
```

This mirrors the existing filter in `use-organization-occurrences.ts` which already has an `OccurrenceFilter` mechanism for this purpose.
close_reason: d3c1892 fix: exclude HumanObservation records from species predictions panel

title: Fix 40.5+40.6: extractCid triplicated across three files — extract to shared utility
description:
## Review of green_globe-40.5 and green_globe-40.6 found: extractCid duplicated three times

The `extractCid` helper function is now copy-pasted identically in three separate files:

1. `src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/ProjectOverlay/store/utils.ts` (lines 53-74) — original
2. `src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_hooks/use-organization-measured-trees.ts` (lines 88-105) — added by 40.5
3. `src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_hooks/use-organization-occurrences.ts` (lines 152-169) — added by 40.6
4. `src/app/(map-routes)/(main)/_components/ProjectOverlay/Biodiversity/Predictions/store/utils.ts` (lines 147-164) — added by 40.6

All four implementations are byte-for-byte identical. The spec for 40.6 explicitly called out 'see extractCid() in store/utils.ts lines 53-74 for the pattern' — meaning the intent was to reuse, not copy.

## Risk
Any future bug fix or CID format change must be applied in four places. This has already caused one bug (the com.atProto case bug in 40.8 was a similar copy-paste divergence).

## Fix
Export `extractCid` from `store/utils.ts` (or move it to `@/lib/atproto/sdk-utils` if one exists) and import it in the three other files. Remove the three duplicate definitions.

title: Fix 40.5: fetchMeasuredTreeOccurrences fetches ALL occurrences then filters client-side — O(N) unnecessary data transfer
description:
## Review of green_globe-40.5 found: full occurrence collection fetched before filtering to measured trees

`fetchMeasuredTreeOccurrences` in `use-organization-measured-trees.ts` (lines 207-232) paginates through the entire `app.gainforest.dwc.occurrence` collection for an org, then filters client-side to `basisOfRecord === 'HumanObservation'` + `dynamicProperties.dataType === 'measuredTree'`.

For orgs with large species prediction datasets (e.g. 3,477 Restor records), this means downloading all species occurrence records over the network just to discard them. The `com.atproto.repo.listRecords` XRPC endpoint does not support server-side filtering, so this is unavoidable with the current ATProto API — but the parallel fetch of measurements (also full collection) compounds the problem.

## Evidence
`use-organization-measured-trees.ts` lines 207-232: no filter parameter passed to `listRecords`, all records fetched then filtered at lines 235-247.

## Context / Mitigation
This is a known ATProto limitation (no server-side filtering on listRecords). The correct long-term fix is to store measured trees in a separate collection (e.g. `app.gainforest.dwc.measuredTree`) or use a dedicated index record. Short-term, the fallback to `null` when no measured trees are found (line 249) means non-migrated orgs pay this cost unnecessarily on every site activation.

## Fix options (for Lead to decide)
1. Add a lightweight 'has measured trees' flag to the org record to skip the fetch entirely for non-migrated orgs
2. Accept the current behavior as a known limitation and document it
3. Move measured trees to a separate ATProto collection

File this as a performance issue for the Lead to triage — do not block 40.5 on it.

title: Fix 40.5: MeasuredTreeOccurrence type defined in types.ts but never used — dead type
description:
## Review of green_globe-40.5 found: MeasuredTreeOccurrence type is dead code

The spec for 40.5 asked for a `MeasuredTreeOccurrence` type to be added to `store/types.ts`. It was added (lines 169-174) but is never imported or used anywhere in the codebase. The hook (`use-organization-measured-trees.ts`) builds `NormalizedTreeFeature` objects directly without going through `MeasuredTreeOccurrence`.

The type also declares `_source` and `_occurrenceUri` fields that are never populated on any feature — so even if the type were used, those fields would always be undefined at runtime.

## Evidence
- `store/types.ts` lines 169-174: type defined
- `use-organization-measured-trees.ts`: imports `NormalizedTreeFeature` and `MeasuredTreesGeoJSON` from types.ts, but NOT `MeasuredTreeOccurrence`
- No other file imports `MeasuredTreeOccurrence`

## Fix
Either:
a) Remove `MeasuredTreeOccurrence` from types.ts (it adds no value if unused), OR
b) Use it as the intermediate type in the hook's mapping step and populate `_source` / `_occurrenceUri` for future debuggability

Option (a) is simpler. Option (b) is only worth doing if the `_occurrenceUri` field will be used by a future feature (e.g. deep-linking to the PDS record).

title: Epic: Adopt Audubon Core for biodiversity media on ATProto PDS
description:
## Goal
Replace the hardcoded evidence fields on dwc.occurrence (trunkEvidence, leafEvidence, barkEvidence, imageEvidence, audioEvidence, videoEvidence, spectrogramEvidence) with a standards-aligned Audubon Core multimedia record type. Each media item becomes its own record linked to an occurrence, using the TDWG ac:subjectPart controlled vocabulary.

## Context
- Epic green_globe-40 added trunkEvidence/leafEvidence/barkEvidence to dwc.occurrence, but this approach is rigid (only 3 photo types), non-standard, and caused blob GC issues
- Audubon Core (AC) is the official TDWG/GBIF standard for biodiversity media metadata — the companion to Darwin Core
- AC has a controlled vocabulary (ac:subjectPart) with terms for bark, leaf, entireOrganism, flower, fruit, seed, stem, etc.
- Migration scripts from green_globe-40 have NOT been run in production yet — no data to migrate
- Frontend tasks from green_globe-40 (40.5, 40.6) are done but read from fields that will be replaced

## Key Decisions
1. Create new lexicon: app.gainforest.ac.multimedia — one record per media item
2. Each AC record links to a dwc.occurrence via occurrenceRef (at-uri)
3. Use ac:subjectPart controlled values: entireOrganism, leaf, bark, flower, fruit, seed, stem
4. Remove trunkEvidence/leafEvidence/barkEvidence from dwc.occurrence (added in 40.1, never populated in production)
5. Keep imageEvidence on dwc.occurrence for now (backward compat for species predictions already in production) — migrate to AC in a follow-up
6. Migration scripts use naturally atomic pattern: uploadBlob → createRecord (one AC record per blob)
7. Compress images to 800px wide WebP q80 (~340KB avg) — same as green_globe-40
8. Frontend reads AC records via listRecords filtered by occurrenceRef

## Supersedes
- green_globe-40.1 (lexicon fields — will be reverted)
- green_globe-40.3/40.12 (tree migration script — will be rewritten)
- green_globe-40.4/40.13 (species image migration — will be rewritten)
- green_globe-40.5 (frontend measured trees — will be updated)
- green_globe-40.6 (frontend species images — will be updated)
- green_globe-40.7, 40.10, 40.11 (cancelled cleanup tasks)

## Success Criteria
- New app.gainforest.ac.multimedia lexicon exists with subjectPart, file, occurrenceRef fields
- trunkEvidence/leafEvidence/barkEvidence removed from dwc.occurrence
- Migration scripts create AC records (one per photo) linked to occurrence records
- Frontend displays tree photos and species images from AC records
- All media blobs are naturally atomic (one blob per record, no GC risk)

title: Rewrite measured tree migration script to create AC multimedia records
description:
## Files
- scripts/migrations/migrate-measured-trees-to-ac-records.js (create)

## What to do
Create a NEW migration script that creates `app.gainforest.ac.multimedia` records for measured tree photos instead of writing evidence fields on `dwc.occurrence`.

This script replaces `migrate-measured-trees-to-dwc-occurrences.js` (from 40.3/40.12). The old script should NOT be modified or deleted — it stays as a reference.

### Architecture
For each tree with photos:
1. The `dwc.occurrence` record already exists (or will be created) with `basisOfRecord: 'HumanObservation'` and `dynamicProperties.dataType: 'measuredTree'`. The occurrence record does NOT get any evidence blob fields.
2. For each photo (trunk, leaf, bark), create a separate `app.gainforest.ac.multimedia` record with:
   - `occurrenceRef`: AT-URI of the dwc.occurrence record (`at://<did>/app.gainforest.dwc.occurrence/<rkey>`)
   - `subjectPart`: `'entireOrganism'` for trunk photos, `'leaf'` for leaf photos, `'bark'` for bark photos
   - `file`: compressed blob (800px wide WebP q80 via sharp)
   - `format`: `'image/webp'`
   - `accessUri`: original S3/Kobo URL (full-res reference)
   - `variantLiteral`: `'Medium Quality'`
   - `createdAt`: ISO timestamp

### Flow per tree feature
1. Extract photo URLs from GeoJSON properties (same logic as existing script: `extractPhotoUrls()`)
2. Compute deterministic occurrence rkey: `makeOccurrenceRkey(orgDid, lat, lon, species)` — same as existing script
3. Check if occurrence record exists; if not, create it (same fields as existing script, but WITHOUT any evidence blob fields)
4. For each photo URL (trunk, leaf, bark) that is not null and not a Google Drive URL:
   a. Download the image
   b. Compress to 800px WebP q80
   c. `uploadBlob` to PDS
   d. Immediately `createRecord` for `app.gainforest.ac.multimedia` referencing the blob — this is the atomic pattern that prevents blob GC
   e. Delete local temp file
5. Create `dwc.measurement` records for DBH and height (same as existing script)

### Idempotency
- Use deterministic rkeys for AC records: `ac${sha256(occurrenceRkey + '::' + subjectPart).slice(0, 20)}`
- Before creating, check if AC record with that rkey already exists
- Progress tracking: `scripts/migrations/data/ac-measured-trees-progress.json`
- Error log: `scripts/migrations/data/ac-measured-trees-errors.json`

### CLI interface
Same as existing script:
```
node scripts/migrations/migrate-measured-trees-to-ac-records.js   [--service https://climateai.org]   [--repos-file tmp/pds-repo-inventory-*.json]   [--credentials tmp/pds-credentials.json]   [--handle handle.climateai.org]   [--limit N]   [--dry-run]   [--out tmp/ac-measured-trees-migration-results.json]
```

### Key differences from old script
- Does NOT write `trunkEvidence`/`leafEvidence`/`barkEvidence` on occurrence records
- Creates separate `app.gainforest.ac.multimedia` records instead
- Each photo = one `uploadBlob` + one `createRecord` (naturally atomic)
- AC record rkeys are deterministic based on occurrence rkey + subjectPart

### Reuse from existing script
Copy these helper functions verbatim from `migrate-measured-trees-to-dwc-occurrences.js`:
- `parseArgs()` (update defaults for new file paths)
- `loadInventory()`, `loadCredentials()`
- `loadProgress()`, `saveProgress()`, `loadErrors()`, `appendError()`
- `fetchBinaryWithRetry()`, `fetchBinary()`, `fetchJson()`
- `getSharp()`, `compressImage()`
- `makeOccurrenceRkey()`, `makeMeasurementRkey()`
- `extractSpecies()`, `extractEventDate()`, `extractDbh()`, `extractHeight()`, `extractPhotoUrls()`, `isGoogleDriveUrl()`
- `listSiteRecords()`, `fetchPdsBlob()`
- `listExistingOccurrenceRkeys()`, `listExistingMeasurementRkeys()`

## Don't
- Don't modify the existing `migrate-measured-trees-to-dwc-occurrences.js`
- Don't modify any lexicon files or frontend code
- Don't use `putRecord` to update occurrence records with evidence fields — the whole point is that evidence goes in AC records
- Don't buffer multiple blobs in memory — stream one at a time (12GB disk constraint)
acceptance_criteria:
1. scripts/migrations/migrate-measured-trees-to-ac-records.js exists
2. Script creates app.gainforest.ac.multimedia records (not evidence fields on occurrence)
3. Each AC record has: occurrenceRef (at-uri), subjectPart (entireOrganism|leaf|bark), file (blob), format, accessUri, variantLiteral, createdAt
4. Blob upload is immediately followed by createRecord (atomic pattern)
5. Deterministic rkeys: ac + sha256(occurrenceRkey + '::' + subjectPart).slice(0, 20)
6. --dry-run flag works (no PDS writes)
7. Progress file at scripts/migrations/data/ac-measured-trees-progress.json
8. bun run lint passes

title: Rewrite species image migration script to create AC multimedia records
description:
## Files
- scripts/migrations/migrate-species-images-to-ac-records.js (create)

## What to do
Create a NEW migration script that creates `app.gainforest.ac.multimedia` records for species prediction images instead of writing `imageEvidence` blobs on `dwc.occurrence`.

This script replaces `migrate-occurrence-images-to-blobs.js` (from 40.4/40.13). The old script should NOT be modified or deleted.

### Architecture
For each MachineObservation occurrence with a `speciesImageUrl` or `thumbnailUrl`:
1. The `dwc.occurrence` record already exists with `basisOfRecord: 'MachineObservation'`
2. Create one `app.gainforest.ac.multimedia` record with:
   - `occurrenceRef`: AT-URI of the existing dwc.occurrence record
   - `subjectPart`: `'entireOrganism'` (species photos are always whole-organism)
   - `file`: compressed blob (800px wide WebP q80 via sharp)
   - `format`: `'image/webp'`
   - `accessUri`: original `speciesImageUrl` or `thumbnailUrl` (full-res reference)
   - `variantLiteral`: `'Medium Quality'`
   - `createdAt`: ISO timestamp

### Flow per occurrence
1. List all `dwc.occurrence` records for the org
2. Filter to `basisOfRecord === 'MachineObservation'` with `speciesImageUrl` or `thumbnailUrl` present
3. For each qualifying occurrence:
   a. Check if an AC record already exists for this occurrence (deterministic rkey)
   b. Download the image from `speciesImageUrl` (or `thumbnailUrl` fallback)
   c. Compress to 800px WebP q80
   d. `uploadBlob` to PDS
   e. Immediately `createRecord` for `app.gainforest.ac.multimedia` (atomic pattern)

### Idempotency
- Deterministic rkeys: `ac${sha256(occurrenceUri + '::entireOrganism').slice(0, 20)}`

- Don't modify any lexicon files or frontend code

title: Fix: layer.json name and description fields missing maxGraphemes (from green_globe-1.5)
description: Review of green_globe-1.5 found that organization/layer.json has two pre-existing string fields without maxGraphemes constraints: (1) 'name' field: type string, no maxGraphemes, no format — the spec says this is the layer name but no length constraint was added. (2) 'description' field: type string, no maxGraphemes, no format. AC9 requires 'all string fields have maxGraphemes constraints'. These are pre-existing fields that the task did not fix. All new fields added by the task (category, opacity, unit, minValue, maxValue, tilePattern, bounds, dataSource, dataDate, propertyKey) do have correct maxGraphemes constraints. The legendEntry def fields also have correct constraints.

title: Fix: occurrence.json and dwc/defs.json plantTraits duplicate plant trait fields (from green_globe-1.6 / green_globe-1.8)
description: Review of green_globe-1.6 and green_globe-1.8 found a second cross-task duplication. Task 1.8 defined a plantTraits def in dwc/defs.json with fields: woodDensity, maxHeight, stemDiameter, stemConduitDiameter, barkThickness, rootDepth, leafArea, specificLeafArea, seedMass, growthForm, leafType, dispersalMode, pollinationMode, edibleParts, economicUses, traitSource. Task 1.6 independently added plant trait fields inline on occurrence.json: woodDensity, treeMaxHeight (note: different name from plantTraits.maxHeight), stemDiameter, stemConduitDiameter, barkThickness, rootDepth, edibleParts, economicUses. This creates: (1) duplication of 6 fields between occurrence.json inline and dwc.defs#plantTraits; (2) naming inconsistency — occurrence.json uses 'treeMaxHeight' but dwc.defs#plantTraits uses 'maxHeight' for the same concept. The occurrence.json should reference dwc.defs#plantTraits via a ref field, or the shared def should be removed. This will cause confusion for SDK consumers.

title: Fix: info.json email field spec says format:uri which is semantically incorrect for email addresses (from green_globe-1.1)
description: Review of green_globe-1.1 spec found a semantic error in the task description itself: the spec says email should have 'format: uri' (string, format: uri, maxGraphemes: 256). Email addresses are NOT URIs — they are not valid URI format values in ATProto's lexicon system. The correct approach is either: (a) use type: string with maxGraphemes: 256 and no format (email is just a string), or (b) use format: 'email' if ATProto supports it. Using format: uri would cause validation failures for any email address that doesn't start with a URI scheme. This is a spec defect that needs to be corrected when task 1.1 is re-implemented. Note: this issue is secondary to green_globe-2 (the fields were never added at all), but the spec itself needs fixing before re-implementation.

title: Epic: Regenerate SDK TypeScript types from enriched lexicons
description:
